basics.name: Jacques Thomazo
basics.label: Battery Systems Technical Lead - Backend Services Developer - 15+ years engineering Battery Systems through simulation and data
basics.summary: Battery systems engineer turned platform builder: 15 years across cell selection, pack design & validation, system sizing and modelling, now building the telemetry and diagnostics platform that turns fleet data into battery engineering decisions.

work.name: Forsee Power
work.summary: Build an IoT pipeline and data platform.
highlights: Implement a remote diagnostic reporting service for telemetry-equipped vehicles, cutting a report from 2 days & €1,000 to 200 seconds & €0.50.
Lead a 6 people cross-functional managers team to implement a company-wide resources SharePoint service (100+ resources).

Define the architecture and technical stack of the internal telemetry platform: component boundaries across a multi-repo toolchain, with versioning and schema evolution rules that keep services compatible as the data model changes.
Build the backend services in Python and FastAPI — REST and WebSocket APIs, Pydantic models published as OpenAPI, MongoDB persistence, and retry and failure handling for an unreliable field network.

Specify diagnostic acquisition over CAN (UDS, J1939, OBD2, DID/DTC) and gateway configuration across a fleet of 100 vehicles (8 customers, 7 products, 2 generations), turning raw fleet signals into a queryable battery dataset.
Set the team's software quality bar and enforce it in CI: ruff formatting and static analysis, ty type checking, pytest, pre-commit.

work.name: Forsee Power
highlights: Reworked SOH estimation and prediction algorithms for the embedded application, bringing accuracy to ±3%.
Built a hybrid Fuel-Cell Battery System model to explore optimised Energy Management Strategies (Matlab/Simulink/Simscape, Git).
Led development of the Next Generation high cell integration density Battery Platform (Cell-to-pack mass ratio >80%).
Delivered in-house manufactured prototype packs (5 units, 17kWh/800V/LTO) for a key railway project.

work.name: McLaren Applied
work.summary: Technical lead on a top tier, high performance racing EV battery concept.
highlights: Simulated and optimised battery system sizing for tender specifications requirements - 43kWh/600kW/284kg/220L/800V.
Defined, tracked and analysed results of a cell performance test plan.

work.name: ARRIVAL
work.summary: Led ISO 26262 functional safety analysis and implementation (HARA, FMEA) across HV battery (ASIL C), steer-by-wire and brake-by-wire products (both ASIL D).

work.name: ARRIVAL
work.summary: Owned the development lifecycle for HV & LV batteries, PCBs and electrical systems: requirements, concepts, design reviews, testing and validation. Modular 2kWh/400V architecture.

work.name: Vantage Power
work.summary: Implemented a long-term (6 months) cell-level test bench (0-5V & 25A/channel × 16 channels) for performance and durability testing, from specifications through to continuous data monitoring pipelines.

work.name: Vantage Power
work.summary: Multidisciplinary battery engineer, tackling from HV components sizing (600V architecture) and selection to small batch battery production (4 × 15kWh battery systems) through 3D prototyping (10-batch PDU enclosure parts, PETG) and Cooling System simulation (dual 15kWh pack system, immersion cooling).

work.name: Renault
work.summary: Built, led and owned battery safety validation (mechanical, thermal, electrical, etc.) for hybrid and electric vehicles battery systems, from 48 to 400V - Projects: Twizy, Fluence, Kangoo, Zoe Gen 1 & 2, 48V LTO mild-hybrid battery.

work.name: EDF R&D — IRDEP
work.summary: Synthesis and characterisation of the zinc-oxide front layer of CIGS thin-film photovoltaic cells.
highlights: Electrochemical synthesis in aqueous solution.
Characterisation of the resulting layers (Hall effect, electrochemical impedance, four-point resistivity, Schottky-diode impedance).
Characterisation of the completed cells.

work.name: LANXESS — Ion Exchange Resins
work.summary: Design, sizing and development of a laboratory production rig for monodisperse beads.
highlights: Studied microfluidic production approaches.
Sized the system and ran lab tests with microfluidic chips.
Implemented the continuous-flow process steps downstream of the chip.

work.name: Chimie ParisTech — LCMCP
work.summary: Research on non-linear optical materials (YAB) at the Laboratoire de Chimie de la Matière Condensée de Paris.
highlights: Solid-state materials synthesis.
Materials characterisation (microscopy, XRD, DSC, TGA).
Laser testing.

work.name: Hitex
work.summary: Optimisation of an industrial-scale extraction process.
highlights: Worked autonomously on a batch process across double-shift operation (extraction, decantation, filtration, evaporative concentration, drying).
Proposed process improvements.

education.institution: École Nationale Supérieure de Chimie de Paris (Chimie ParisTech)
education.area: Renewable Energies, Chemistry, Electrochemistry, Physical Chemistry, Semiconductors

publications.name: A Partnership between Renault and French First Responders to Ensure Safe Intervention on Crash or Fire-Damaged Electrical Vehicles
